author: @ashadizanur4907
text: great videos sir. really great inspiration for energy generation. next to find water generation ideas

author: @Noswiatel
text: Vacuum cleaners cable winding mechanism would make quite a spectacular clockwork battery.

author: @zylascope
text: Strange noise at 4:10? Aliens spying on human technology?

author: @Alexander_Sannikov
text: i don't like it that the actual test video segment is slowed down without even mentioning it or saying how much it's actually slowed.

author: @Route72Trucks
text: Always a blast Robert!  Your input on things is greatly appreciated and valued.     I wander if we could get your thoughts on a gravity type machine?   Heavy counterweight geared to turn a generator.   I’ve had a few things rolling around in my head for a while now.

author: @tebbi67
text:
This can only mean that the torque of the movement is high and the speed is small. . maybe a stepper motor would be a better generator that has more magnetic fields per revolution that can deliver voltage.
But thx for the vid That also interested me if anything comes out at all!

author: @albertglej770
text: Wow. I just lubricated the exact same fan yesterday. I think it's a shame. This fan is really good and shines nicely. :-)

replies.author: @albertglej770
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering :-D  
Can I ask? Where are you taking useful waste?

author: @aquarionh2o132
text: Cute!  Reminds me of making a gravity powered generator.  Use weights like a clock to power a generator.  1500kg should do it.😎👍

author: @seychellesroland1399
text: What no whistling. Like HOMMER WOULD SAID DOOH.

author: @cdorcey1735
text: I think I might rather have a clock-work fan to cool my face on a hot day when I'm away from electricity.  Leave the fan blades ON!  It must be more efficient that waving my beefy arms around with a Japanese fan.

author: @ExtantFrodo2
text: Did you neglect to mention the need to remove the escapement mechanism so that it could free wheel?

author: @MikeGrayM70
text: I wonder if weights could be used instead of a wind up spring. Like old clocks whose weights have to be pulled back up to keep them running.

replies.author: @77pete7
replies.text: Yes Mike ... I was just checking to see if anyone had mentioned it before telling Rob! There is a device produced with a wind up weight to provide light for kids to do their homework in areas without electricity!

author: @pulesjet
text: I'm thinking the CPU Fan was not the best choice on this one.  They're wired for ultra low current.  Try this with a toy car motor.

author: @thornhedge9639
text: Robert you're a real Gem.  You never run out of fuel for the imagination...  Thanks.............................Tim

author: @Frossglass
text: this is a great idea for a camping lamp or something portable

author: @markhodgson2348
text: Show this to "Click spring" the YouTube clock maker

author: @gusmartin6053
text: The return spring in a tape measure works well for a DIY windup mechanism. It has a fair amount of force and turns many more revolutions than the little toy mechanisms.

author: @markhodgson2348
text: Brilliant easy to scale up

author: @geodeaholicm4889
text: if you add a joule thief circuit to it the led wood light.

author: @victoryfirst2878
text: A real interesting way to make power. Low for sure but the logic is correct. Keep up the great work Robert. Have a good day too. VF

author: @AtlasReburdened
text: It's a shame that springs can't hold more power.

author: @jasonwitt8619
text: Did your son or grandson put you up to this, sounds like a good school project for 10th or 11th grade. I can picture you fooling around in the shop and going, oh wait a minute guess what we can build, lol, nice.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yep lol - that's exactly where this came from lol - that is most perceptive mate - but still we have to have fun too lol

replies.author: @jasonwitt8619
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering LOL, it is scary how much you and I are alike. Yep must have fun. Cheers mate

author: @wayne6220
text: Very entertaining, thank you. It reminds me of those clock work wind up radios that were around years ago.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yeah me too - it's the same basic mechanism

author: @Scott_C
text: Next use a grandfather's clock!  Converting pendulum swing/spring to electricity.  Sounds awesome for that steam punk project someone is working on.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I just love steam punk mate

author: @sofa-lofa4241
text:
Nice illustration of how to convert energy in a simple way,
I'm off to ebay to order my 'Big Ben to 5V phone charger, pocket size, no battery required (actual size may vary)' while stocks last!

author: @The-Green-Manali-shi
text: Ingenious!

author: @doctorolo
text: this is extremely interesting and although not particularly practical it's a cool idea that shows what you can do with a little clockwork

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's fun educational and easy - what more could you want to do with the kids mate lol

replies.author: @doctorolo
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering indeed stuff like that could help them learn about the basics of energy storage

replies.author: @lloydy321
replies.text: A few years ago I had a few clockwork items. They have since been discarded. Now I need to hunt for more, Typical, lol.

replies.author: @doctorolo
replies.text: @lloydy321 yeah people might call you a hoarder but  you never know when you might need certain thing so you might as well never throw stuff out

replies.author: @boblewis5558
replies.text: You never heard of the late Trevor Baylis CBE and his clockwork radios Robert? He invented them specifically for use in Africa and third world countries where replacing batteries was expensive and difficult due to lack of supply in the bush! Google for him. He died two years ago.

author: @dgpreston5593
text: Neat!
